errors.formattedMessage:
Warning: It looks like you are using '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)'.
Using '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)' is deprecated and strongly discouraged!
The resolc compiler uses a heuristic to detect '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)' calls,
which disables call re-entrancy and supplies all remaining gas instead of the 2300 gas stipend.
However, detection is not guaranteed. You are advised to carefully test this, employ
re-entrancy guards or use the withdrawal pattern instead!
Learn more on https://docs.soliditylang.org/en/latest/security-considerations.html#reentrancy
and https://docs.soliditylang.org/en/latest/common-patterns.html#withdrawal-from-contracts

--> contracts/Kodapay.sol

Warning: It looks like you are using '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)'.
Using '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)' is deprecated and strongly discouraged!
The resolc compiler uses a heuristic to detect '<address payable>.send/transfer(<X>)' calls,
which disables call re-entrancy and supplies all remaining gas instead of the 2300 gas stipend.
However, detection is not guaranteed. You are advised to carefully test this, employ

documentation.text:
@dev Contract module that helps prevent reentrant calls to a function.
 Inheriting from `ReentrancyGuard` will make the {nonReentrant} modifier
 available, which can be applied to functions to make sure there are no nested
 (reentrant) calls to them.
 Note that because there is a single `nonReentrant` guard, functions marked as
 `nonReentrant` may not call one another. This can be worked around by making
 those functions `private`, and then adding `external` `nonReentrant` entry
 points to them.
 TIP: If you would like to learn more about reentrancy and alternative ways
 to protect against it, check out our blog post
 https://blog.openzeppelin.com/reentrancy-after-istanbul/[Reentrancy After Istanbul].

@dev Prevents a contract from calling itself, directly or indirectly.
 Calling a `nonReentrant` function from another `nonReentrant`
 function is not supported. It is possible to prevent this from happening
 by making the `nonReentrant` function external, and making it call a
 `private` function that does the actual work.

@dev Returns true if the reentrancy guard is currently set to "entered", which indicates there is a
 `nonReentrant` function in the call stack.
@dev Interface of the ERC20 standard as defined in the EIP.

@dev Emitted when the allowance of a `spender` for an `owner` is set by
 a call to {approve}. `value` is the new allowance.
@dev Returns the amount of tokens in existence.
@dev Returns the amount of tokens owned by `account`.

@dev Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be
 allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is
 zero by default.
 This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

@dev Sets `amount` as the allowance of `spender` over the caller's tokens.
 Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded.
 IMPORTANT: Beware that changing an allowance with this method brings the risk
 that someone may use both the old and the new allowance by unfortunate
 transaction ordering. One possible solution to mitigate this race
 condition is to first reduce the spender's allowance to 0 and set the
 desired value afterwards:
 https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/issues/20#issuecomment-263524729
 Emits an {Approval} event.

@dev Moves `amount` tokens from `from` to `to` using the
 allowance mechanism. `amount` is then deducted from the caller's
 allowance.
 Returns a boolean value indicating whether the operation succeeded.
 Emits a {Transfer} event.
Cancel a subscription
Check if a payment is due
Get user's subscription IDs
Get subscription details

Note that because there is a single `nonReentrant` guard, functions marked as `nonReentrant` may not call one another. This can be worked around by making those functions `private`, and then adding `external` `nonReentrant` entry points to them. the immutable USDT token reference for all subscription paymentsSECURITY: Validates token address is not zero to prevent deployment errors","params":{"_usdtToken":"Address of the USDT token contract (must be ERC20 compatible)"}},"createSubscription(address,uint256,uint256)":{"custom:requirements":"receiver must not be zero addressamount must be greater than 0frequency must be greater than 0user must have sufficient vault balance for first payment","details":"Creates subscription struct and adds to user's subscription listSECURITY: Validates all parameters and checks sufficient balance for first payment","params":{"amount":"Amount to pay each period (in USDT's smallest unit, 6 decimals)","frequency":"Time between payments in seconds (e.g., 2592000 for 30 days)","receiver":"Address that will receive the recurring payments"},"returns":{"_0":"subId The unique ID of the created subscription"}},"deposit(uint256)":{"custom:requirements":"User must have approved this contract to spend `amount` tokensAmount must be greater than 0User must have sufficient USDT balance","details":"Transfers tokens from user to contract and updates internal balanceSECURITY: Uses nonReentrant modifier to prevent reentrancy attacksSECURITY: Follows Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern for safety","params":{"amount":"Amount of USDT tokens to deposit (in token's smallest unit, 6 decimals)"}},"executePayment(uint256)":{"custom:incentive":"Executor earns 1% fee, creating economic incentive for execution","custom:incentives":"Executor receives 1% of payment amount as rewardCreates self-sustaining ecosystem where payments never miss","custom:security":"Critical function with multiple validation layers","custom:temporal":"Uses block.timestamp (more reliable on Polkadot ~6s blocks)First payment executable immediately (lastPayment = 0)Subsequent payments require: block.timestamp >= lastPayment + frequency","details":"Core function of incentivized execution system - anyone can call thisSECURITY: Uses nonReentrant modifier and updates timestamp before transfersSECURITY: Validates subscription is active and payment is due","params":{"subId":"The subscription ID to execute payment for"},"returns":{"_0":"bool Returns true if payment was executed successfully"}},"withdraw(uint256)":{"custom:gas-optimization":"Balance is checked before transfer to save gas on failure","custom:requirements":"Amount must be greater than 0User must have sufficient vault balance","custom:security":"Uses nonReentrant to prevent reentrancy attacks","details":"Transfers tokens from contract to user and updates internal balanceSECURITY: Uses nonReentrant modifier to prevent reentrancy attacksSECURITY: Updates balance BEFORE external call (Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern)","params":{"amount":"Amount of USDT tokens to withdraw (in token's smallest unit, 6 decimals)"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"cancelSubscription(uint256)":{"notice":"Cancel a subscription"},"constructor":{"notice":"Initializes the Kodapay contract with the USDT user's vault for subscription payments"},"executePayment(uint256)":{"notice":"Executes a subscription payment and rewards the executor with 1% fee"},"getSubscription(uint256)":{"notice":"Get subscription details"},"getUserSubscriptions(address)":{"notice":"Get user's subscription IDs"},"isPaymentDue(uint256)":{"notice":"Check if a payment is due"},"withdraw(uint256)":{"notice":"Withdraws USDT tokens from the user's vault back to their
